quiz_items.question: Which laser class is considered hazardous for scattered (diffuse) reflection exposure?
quiz_items.explanation: Class IV exceeds Class IIIb limits and is a hazard for scattered (diffuse) reflection as well as direct exposure. Classes II and IIIa are primarily direct viewing hazards.

quiz_items.question: Class IIIa products emitting visible light have a maximum total collectable radiant power of ______ milliwatts.
quiz_items.explanation: Class IIIa limits apply to visible spectrum products where the total collectable radiant power does not exceed 5 milliwatts. This class includes most helium-neon lasers.

quiz_items.question: For Class IIIb products, which hazards exist? (Select all that apply)
options: Ocular hazards for direct exposure throughout the class range
Skin hazards at higher levels of the class
quiz_items.explanation: Class IIIb emission levels (5-500 mW visible) are ocular hazards for direct exposure throughout the range and skin hazards at the higher levels. Scattered reflection hazards begin at Class IV. While high powers may present fire hazards, this is not specifically mentioned in the regulatory classification criteria.
